name: What's Your Leadership Style?
description: Discover your natural approach to leading teams and organizations

visionary.title: The Visionary Leader
visionary.description: You focus on the big picture and inspire others with future possibilities. Excellent at guiding organizations through change, you help teams see beyond immediate challenges to long-term success.
visionary.strengths: Innovation, inspiration, change leadership, strategic direction
visionary.challenges: May sometimes overlook operational details or immediate needs

democratic.title: The Democratic Leader
democratic.description: You believe in collaborative decision-making and team input. By valuing diverse perspectives, you create inclusive environments where everyone feels heard and valued.
democratic.strengths: Team morale, diverse input, employee engagement, conflict resolution
democratic.challenges: May sometimes struggle with quick decisions in urgent situations

coaching.title: The Coaching Leader
coaching.description: You focus on developing individuals' potential and fostering growth. Your supportive approach helps team members build confidence and expand their capabilities.
coaching.strengths: Talent development, employee retention, skill-building, motivation
coaching.challenges: May sometimes invest time in team members who aren't receptive

pace-setting.title: The Pace-Setting Leader
pace-setting.description: You lead by example with high standards and achievement focus. Your drive for excellence motivates teams to deliver quality results and continuous improvement.
pace-setting.strengths: Excellence, productivity, quality standards, goal achievement
pace-setting.challenges: May sometimes create pressure without adequate support

questions.question: Your primary leadership focus is on:
options: Setting a compelling direction for the future
Achieving excellent results through personal example

questions.question: When making important decisions, you:
options: Gather input from the whole team
Think about how it will help individuals grow
Rely on your own expertise and standards

questions.question: Your team would describe you as:
When problems arise, you typically:
options: Refocus everyone on the long-term goals
Facilitate a team discussion to solve it together
Use it as a teaching moment for development
Jump in to demonstrate how to fix it properly

questions.question: In meetings, you're most likely to:
options: Talk about long-term strategy and vision
Ensure everyone gets equal speaking time
Ask questions to draw out people's thoughts
Provide clear direction based on your expertise
